title: Zero-config defaults: herdctl (no --config/--demo) auto-creates working config + persistent dev web token, runs against real herdr
action: PBI-004. When run with neither --config nor --demo: resolve default config path ~/.config/herdr-gateway/config.json; if missing, create it with sane defaults (herdr_session 'default', herdr_socket default ~/.config/herdr/herdr.sock, allowed_roots [~/projects if exists else ~], bind_addr 127.0.0.1:8787, static_dir 'static'); then load it. Web token: if HERDCTL_WEB_SECRET env unset, read ~/.config/herdr-gateway/herdctl.env; if it lacks HERDCTL_WEB_SECRET, generate a random hex token, persist to that file (mode 600), and use it. Print the token + URL on startup. Keep fail-closed (empty allowed_roots still errors). Serious/standard, not a demo hack.

truths: herdctl with no args runs against real herdr using an auto-created config
web token auto-generated + persisted mode 600 if unset
prohibitions: Do not read the operator's real secrets beyond the gateway's own env file

verify: cargo test --quiet config && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings

trace.outcome: Zero-config: herdctl no-args auto-creates config.json (sane defaults, fail-closed root) + persistent token in herdctl.env (mode 600), prints token+URL, runs vs real herdr. Live-verified. Committed 75dad3a.
trace.verification_evidence: Live no-arg run: created config.json (bind 127.0.0.1:8787, session default, allowed_roots [~/projects]) + herdctl.env mode 600 with generated token, printed token, served /api/health proto16.
trace.verify_output: 11 config tests; live no-arg run auto-created config+token(600), served real herdr
